title: Excited states engineering maximizes singlet generation by triplet fusion in conjugated systems
authors: Alessandra Ronchi, Masashi Mamada, Michel Frigoli, Angelo Monguzzi
abstract: Photon upconverters are anti Stokes emitters capable of generating photons with higher energy than those absorbed. This behavior can be achieved through different mechanisms, which are extensively studied for applications in imaging, anticounterfeiting, information encryption and most importantly, solar energy technologies. Among these mechanisms, photon upconversion based on sensitized triplet-triplet annihilation (sTTA-UC) is particularly attractive because it operates under low intensity, incoherent light. In sTTA UC, two optically dark triplet states of a conjugated annihilator fuse upon collision to populate a higher energy fluorescent singlet, with the triplets initially generated via energy transfer from a lower energy sensitizer.   Here we introduce a general molecular design strategy to maximize singlet generation through TTA. By selective substitution, we engineered a naphthalene derived annihilator with an excited state energy landscape that strongly favors singlet formation, achieving yields up to 0.83. When combined with an appropriate triplet sensitizer, the system delivers stable UV/visible upconversion peaking at 390 nm, with an absolute upconversion yield about 0.19 and an activation excitation intensity threshold lower than 0.1 suns under non-coherent broadband excitation fully compatible with the requirements of solar powered technologies.
summary: The researchers developed a way to maximize the production of high-energy photons from low-energy light by engineering the electronic structure of organic molecules. By tuning the energy levels to favor singlet formation during triplet collisions, they achieved high upconversion yields compatible with solar energy technologies.
main_problem: The efficiency of sensitized triplet-triplet annihilation upconversion (sTTA-UC) is limited by a low statistical factor (f) for singlet formation, often due to energy loss through accessible triplet states.
main_result: By engineering the excited-state energy landscape of a naphthalene-derived annihilator, the authors achieved a singlet generation yield (f) of 0.83 and an absolute upconversion yield of 0.19.
method: The study combines molecular engineering via selective substitution, DFT/TD-DFT calculations to model the energy landscape, and experimental spectroscopy (steady-state and time-resolved) to validate the TTA process.
model_or_system: A dual-dye solution consisting of an iridium complex sensitizer, Ir(ppy)2(acac), and a naphthalene-derived annihilator, TIPS-Ph-Naph, operating via Dexter energy transfer and triplet-triplet annihilation.
key_observables: Photon conversion quantum yield (phi_uc), statistical factor (f), upconversion peak wavelength, excitation intensity threshold (I_th), and triplet lifetime.
important_parameters: The transition from quadratic to linear power dependency, the activation threshold of 0.71 mW/cm2 under broadband excitation, and the resonance between the T1T1 complex and high-energy Sn states.
main_assumptions: The design assumes that tuning the energy landscape to favor resonances with high-energy singlets is more effective than the traditional approach of avoiding all resonances.
figures_summary: Fig 1a shows the sTTA-UC photophysical process; Fig 4a compares absorption and excitation spectra; Fig 4b shows the upconversion intensity vs. excitation intensity, illustrating the quadratic-to-linear transition and solar compatibility.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the sTTA-UC efficiency bottleneck, describes the molecular design strategy and synthesis of the engineered annihilator, presents computational modeling of the energy landscape, provides experimental validation of the high singlet yield and upconversion efficiency, and concludes with the system's performance under solar-simulated conditions.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: Fig. 1 | General photophysics of sensitized triplet-triplet annihilation based photon upconversion (sTTA-UC). a) Following photon absorption, a light harvester/triplet sensitizer molecule with absorption coefficient α is promoted to its excited singlet state (S1*) that efficiently undergoes intersystem crossing (ISC) to its triplet state T1 (Tsens). Forward energy transfer (ET) occurs and competes with back-energy transfer (back-ET) to the annihilator/emitter triplet state T1. This can either spontaneously decay or undergo triplet–triplet annihilation (TTA). The triplets collisional complex <T1T1> can relax to the emitter S1 state, which then emits high energy light when relaxing to the...

The PL spectrum was acquired under cw excitation at 320 nm. The inset reports the PL intensity decay trace at 390 nm recorded under pulsed laser excitation at 340 nm.
